name: Kubernetes MCP Server
what_it_does: Community MCP server for Kubernetes enabling AI agents to interact with K8s clusters — querying pod/deployment/service state, reading logs, describing resources, managing namespaces, and performing kubectl-equivalent operations.
use_cases: Querying pod and deployment status for infrastructure monitoring agents
Reading pod logs for incident investigation from on-call agents
Checking resource utilization across namespaces for capacity planning
Describing failing deployments for automated root cause analysis
Managing Kubernetes resources from GitOps and deployment agents
not_for: Production cluster modifications without human review
best_when: An agent needs to investigate or monitor a Kubernetes cluster — reading resource state, logs, and events for operations or debugging workflows.
avoid_when: You need cluster creation or infrastructure provisioning — use IaC tools like Terraform or Pulumi.

auth.notes: Kubernetes RBAC controls access. Service Account with ClusterRole binding for agents. kubeconfig for local development. RBAC at namespace or cluster level.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source — free for self-hosted; managed K8s on cloud providers has infrastructure costs
pricing.paid_tiers: None (community MCP; K8s cluster costs depend on cloud provider)
pricing.notes: MCP server and Kubernetes itself are free open source. Cluster infrastructure costs are separate.

performance.rate_limits: Kubernetes API server rate limits configurable per cluster

known_agent_gotchas: RBAC setup is critical — agents should have read-only access by default
Namespace scoping is important — cluster-wide queries can be slow and broad
Pod log tailing requires separate streaming endpoint — pagination for historical logs
CRDs (Custom Resources) require separate discovery — not all resources are built-in
Community MCP — stability not guaranteed, may need updates with K8s API changes
Cluster version differences mean some APIs vary — check K8s version compatibility
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced on API server. RBAC is excellent — fine-grained namespace and verb-level access. Service accounts for agents with least-privilege bindings. Community MCP — review code before deployment to production clusters.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Kubernetes RBAC is one of the finest-grained access control systems available. Service account bindings with least privilege. Community MCP requires code review.
